sections.heading: Executive Summary
sections.content:
Isale Investment Limited is pleased to submit this proposal to National Bank of Commerce Limited (NBC) in response to the Request for Proposal (RFP) for a Commercial Credit Origination, Assessment & Workflow Platform.

Isale Investment Limited (formerly Leeds Company Limited) is a Tanzanian-registered ICT services and business consulting firm incorporated on 1st October 2018 (Registration No. 137771411). The company holds a Payment Service Provider (PSP) licence (NBPSL No. 0000-70) issued by the Bank of Tanzania, valid through 25th August 2030, and specializes in banking technology solutions, enterprise software implementation, and digital transformation.

We propose implementing a comprehensive, SaaS-based Commercial Credit Origination and Assessment Platform that addresses all nine functional modules specified in the RFP: Entity Management, Financial Analysis, PD Risk Grading, Credit Proposal Workflow, Deal Structuring, Role-Based Access Control, Integration, Alerts & Notifications, and Training.

Our solution is built on proven credit risk management platforms with deep configurability, designed to align with NBC's target operating model while ensuring scalability across all business segments — Corporate, General Banking, Agriculture, Insurance, and Financial Institutions.

sections.heading: Company Profile
sections.content:
Isale Investment Limited (formerly Leeds Company Limited) is a Tanzanian-registered ICT services and business consulting firm incorporated on 1st October 2018 (Registration No. 137771411). The company specializes in banking technology solutions, enterprise software implementation, digital transformation, and business strategy consulting.

Isale holds a Payment Service Provider (PSP) licence (NBPSL No. 0000-70) issued by the Bank of Tanzania, valid through 25th August 2030, authorizing the provision of payment system services across Mainland Tanzania and Tanzania Zanzibar. The company is VAT-registered (VRN: 40-313410-P) and holds a current ICT Services business licence from Kinondoni Municipal Council.

The company maintains offices in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania and Dubai, UAE (Le Solerium Towers, Dubai Silicon Oasis), enabling delivery across East Africa and the wider region.

Isale's technology division, Isale Digital, delivers banking solutions, business solutions (ERP, CRM, supply chain management), and government digital transformation solutions. The company's banking solutions practice focuses on digital banking platforms, credit risk management systems, security architecture, operational efficiency, and secure financial transaction systems.

sections.heading: Understanding of NBC's Requirements
sections.content:
We understand that NBC seeks to implement a SaaS-based, configurable, secure, and scalable Commercial Credit Origination and Assessment Platform to modernize and streamline its commercial credit lifecycle. The platform must serve NBC's diverse business segments and align with the Bank's target operating model.

The core requirements span nine functional areas:

• Entity Management — Supporting Companies, Bank Financial Institutions, Non-Bank Financial Institutions, Sovereigns, and Individuals (non-borrowing roles), with robust search, creation, duplicate prevention, watchlist tracking, officer assignments, industry coding, shareholding management, and credit/legal hierarchy structures.

• Financial Analysis Module — A chart of account grid for spreading historical financial data and scenario-based projections, standard ratio calculations, and templates for Corporate, General Banking, Agriculture, Insurance/Pension, and Banking FIs. User-Defined Analysis (UDA) capability for custom KPIs and macros.

• PD Risk Grading Module — Directly linked to Financial Analysis for quantitative scoring, qualitative augmentation, Grade/PD mapping, group borrower grading, and sanctioner override capability.

• Credit Proposal Workflow — Full lifecycle from Initiation through Entity updates, Financial Spreading, Risk Grading, Facility/Collateral, LGD analysis, Covenants, Credit Memo, Multi-level Sanctioning, Appeals, Client Response, to Publishing. SLA-based notifications and concurrent workflow prevention.

• Deal Structuring — Facilities and collateral/mitigants management, LGD analysis, covenant management, credit memo generation, multi-option facility (MOF) or standalone product structuring, and underlying asset management.

• Role-Based Access Control — Edit vs. read-only by workflow stage, mandate validations, SSO (SAML/OIDC), RBAC, field-level controls, and comprehensive audit trail.

• Integration — With NBC's core systems including Core Banking & CIF, Identity & Access (SSO), Document Management, Email/SMTP, Credit Data Providers, and Reporting/Analytics (data warehouse/BI).

• Alerts and Notifications — Configurable at client, facility, financial spreading, and covenant levels.

• Training — Comprehensive train-the-trainer approach for effective knowledge transfer.

We further note NBC's emphasis on a phased 2-year implementation, SaaS deployment model, transparent pricing in Tanzania Shillings, and the requirement for 24/7 local support.

sections.heading: Proposed Solution Overview
sections.content:
Isale proposes implementing a best-in-class Commercial Credit Origination and Assessment Platform, leveraging proven credit risk management technology with deep configurability to meet NBC's specific requirements.

Our proposed architecture addresses each functional module:

1. Entity Management Module
— Comprehensive entity lifecycle management supporting five entity categories (Company, Bank FI, Non-Bank FI, Sovereign, Individual) with tailored forms and validation rules
— Advanced search by name, alias, type, country, officers, registration/CIF/legacy IDs
— Duplicate prevention on name+alias+portfolio combination
— Watchlist status tracking (Clean/EWL bands) with proposal-context restrictions
— Officer assignments with mandatory roles, connected parties, and shareholding management (% totals ≤100%)
— Credit and Legal hierarchy management with relationship types, aggregation rules, and anti-circular validation
— Country-specific CIF format validations and legacy ID storage

2. Financial Analysis Module
— Chart of account grid for consistent financial spreading with historical statements and projections
— Pre-built templates: Corporate, General Banking, Agriculture, Insurance/Pension, Banking FIs
— Customizable templates to meet NBC-specific requirements
— User-Defined Analysis (UDA) with entity-level custom KPIs, organizational macros, and report surfacing
— Standard ratio calculations and in-depth financial reporting (BS/IS/Cash Flow/Ratios)
— Controls: target currency defaulting, role-based permissions, statement locking once used in underwriting/ratings
— Optional peer/benchmarking capability

3. PD Risk Grading Module
— Direct integration with Financial Analysis for automated quantitative scoring
— Qualitative question augmentation for comprehensive risk assessment
— Grade/PD mapping to produce final PD Rating per counterparty
— Group borrower grading capability
— Sanctioner override with full audit trail
— Automatic regrade enforcement when financial data changes

4. Credit Proposal Workflow
— Complete workflow: Initiation → Entity Updates → Financial Spreading/Review → Risk Grading → Facility/Collateral → LGD → Covenants → Credit Memo → Multi-level Sanctioning → Appeals → Client Response → Publishing
— Concurrent proposal prevention (block initiation if one is open for entity)
— Edit controls: RG, LGD, Structuring, Covenants, Credit Memo editable only within workflow
— Credit Memo: required pre-sanction, reusable, approval publishes memo and approved changes
— SLA-based notifications for all workflow events

5. Deal Structuring Module
— Facility management with MOF and standalone product structuring
— Collateral and mitigants management with underlying asset tracking
— LGD analysis triggered by facility eligibility rules
— Covenant creation, activation within workflow, and monitoring configuration
— Credit memo generation with configurable templates

6. Security & Access Control
— SSO integration via SAML 2.0 and OIDC
— Granular RBAC with field-level controls
— Edit vs. read-only permissions by workflow stage
— Mandate validations and country alignment for sanctioners
— Comprehensive audit trail for all data changes and approvals

7. Integration Framework
— RESTful APIs and event-driven architecture for all integration points
— Core Banking & CIF: Create/read/update client attributes with country-specific validation
— Document Management: Store credit memos and supporting documents with version control
— Email/SMTP: Configurable notifications and task alerts
— Credit Data Providers: Optional peer/market data ingestion
— Reporting/Analytics: Secure data export for NBC's data warehouse/BI tools

8. Alerts & Notifications Engine
— Configurable at client, facility, financial spreading, and covenant levels
— Event-based email alerts for proposal/task events and overdue SLAs
— Recipient configuration by event type including all participants/assignees

9. Training Program
— Train-the-trainer methodology for sustainable knowledge transfer
— Role-based training modules for different user groups
— Comprehensive documentation and user guides

sections.heading: Implementation Approach
sections.content:
We propose a phased implementation methodology aligned with NBC's 2-year timeline, designed for controlled delivery and incremental value realization.

Phase 0 — Mobilization (Weeks 1-4):
• Project setup, governance framework, and RACI matrix
• Detailed discovery workshops with NBC business SMEs
• Solution design sign-off and environment provisioning
• Integration architecture finalization
• Risk register, communication plan, and change management framework
Deliverables: Project Charter, Solution Design Document, Integration Architecture, Risk Register

Phase 1 — Core Build (Weeks 5-16):
• Entity Management module: all five entity types, search, validation, hierarchy management
• Financial Analysis module: spreading templates (Corporate, General Banking, Agriculture, Insurance, Banking FIs), projections, UDA
• Core workflow engine: initiation, entity updates, financial spreading/review
• Unit testing and first system integration testing cycle
Stage-Gate: SIT 1 sign-off
Deliverables: Entity Management Module, Financial Analysis Module, Core Workflow, SIT1 Report

Phase 2 — Risk & Structuring (Weeks 17-26):
• PD Risk Grading module: quantitative scoring, qualitative augmentation, Grade/PD mapping
• Deal Structuring: facilities, collateral/mitigants, MOF, underlying assets
• LGD Analysis module
• Covenant management
• Credit Memo: configurable templates, generation, approval workflow
• Security & access control: SSO, RBAC, field-level controls, audit trail
Stage-Gate: SIT 2 sign-off
Deliverables: Risk Grading Module, Deal Structuring Module, LGD Module, Covenants, Credit Memo

Phase 3 — Approvals & Publishing (Weeks 27-32):
• Multi-level sanctioning workflow (country → head → committee → group)
• Appeals mechanism
• Client response and publishing controls
• Read/write control enforcement across all modules
• Alerts and notifications engine
Stage-Gate: SIT 3 sign-off
Deliverables: Sanctioning Module, Appeals Module, Publishing Controls, Notifications Engine

Phase 4 — Integrations & Migration (Parallel from Week 10):
• Core Banking & CIF integration
• SSO integration (SAML/OIDC)
• Document Management System integration
• Email/SMTP connector
• Credit Data Provider connectors
• Reporting/BI data export layer
• Data migration from legacy systems
Deliverables: Integration Layer, Migration Scripts, Integration Test Report

Phase 5 — Testing, Training & Go-Live (Weeks 33-40):
• System Integration Testing (full regression)
• User Acceptance Testing with NBC business teams
• Performance and security testing
• Train-the-trainer delivery across all user groups
• Parallel run and production cutover
• Hypercare support (8 weeks post go-live)
Stage-Gate: UAT sign-off, Go-Live approval
Deliverables: UAT Report, Performance Test Results, Security Assessment, Training Materials, Go-Live Checklist

Post Go-Live — Stabilization & Optimization (Weeks 41-104):
• 24/7 support with defined SLAs
• Performance monitoring and optimization
• Ongoing knowledge transfer and capacity building
• Regular system health reviews and upgrade planning

Assumptions:
• NBC will provide a dedicated project team including business SMEs, IT resources, and a Project Sponsor
• Access to NBC's sandbox/development environments within 2 weeks of project initiation
• Third-party integration specifications and access will be provided by NBC
• Change management and organizational readiness activities will be jointly planned and executed
• NBC will provide test data and participate in all testing phases

sections.heading: Team Qualifications
sections.content:
Isale Investment Limited brings together a core team with deep expertise in banking technology, credit risk management, and digital transformation:

• Victor Tesha — Project Director / CEO: MBA (Marketing), BA Social Science. 10+ years in banking across NBC Bank, Azania Bank, UBA Tanzania, and ICB Tanzania. Spearheaded NBC's 2013-2018 strategic turnaround. Expertise in credit and risk management, commercial banking, corporate strategy, product development, and business transformation.

• Prem Ruparelia — Strategic Advisor / Director: MA Corporate Strategy and Governance, MCIPS (UK). 20+ years across procurement, financial services, and consulting in UK, Europe, UAE and East Africa. Blue-chip experience with NatWest Bank, DHL, BAE Systems, Roche Pharmaceuticals. Expertise in investment management, STP, operating model redesign, and large-scale transformation programs.

The team will be augmented with specialist credit risk platform implementation consultants, integration engineers, and data migration specialists as required for the engagement. Detailed CVs and team composition will be provided during the due diligence stage.

sections.heading: Support Model
sections.content:
Isale Investment Limited offers 24/7 local support capability from our Dar es Salaam office:

• Tier 1 — Service Desk (24/7): First-line support for user queries, incident logging, and basic troubleshooting. Response time: 15 minutes for critical, 1 hour for high priority.

• Tier 2 — Application Support (Business Hours + On-Call): Application configuration, workflow adjustments, report customization, and functional issue resolution. Response time: 2 hours for critical, 4 hours for high priority.

• Tier 3 — Engineering Support (Business Hours + On-Call): Platform-level troubleshooting, integration issue resolution, performance optimization, and bug fixes. Response time: 4 hours for critical, 8 hours for high priority.

• Escalation Path: Automated escalation to Tier 2/3 and management based on SLA thresholds.

Service Level Targets:
— Platform Availability: 99.5% uptime (monthly)
— RTO: 4 hours | RPO: 1 hour
— Planned Maintenance: Scheduled during off-peak hours with 72-hour advance notice
— Quarterly Business Reviews with NBC stakeholders
— Annual platform health assessment and upgrade planning

sections.heading: Regulatory Compliance & Security
sections.content:
Our proposed solution is designed to meet Tanzania's regulatory requirements and international security standards:

Regulatory Compliance:
• Bank of Tanzania (BOT) prudential guidelines and reporting requirements
• Tanzania data protection and privacy regulations
• Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Know Your Customer (KYC) compliance
• Credit reporting requirements per BOT directives
• Records retention policies aligned with local regulations

Security Standards:
• ISO 27001 Information Security Management alignment
• OWASP Top 10 security controls in application development
• Data encryption at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+)
• Role-based access control with principle of least privilege
• Comprehensive audit trail for all data changes and access
• Regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing
• Secure development lifecycle (SDLC) practices

Data Protection:
• Data residency within Tanzania (or as directed by NBC)
• Data classification and handling procedures
• Secure data export controls for BI/analytics
• Backup and disaster recovery procedures aligned with NBC BCP

sections.heading: Value Proposition
sections.content:
Isale Investment Limited offers NBC the following differentiated value:

• Local Presence with International Reach: Headquartered in Dar es Salaam with a Dubai office, we provide on-the-ground 24/7 delivery with access to regional and international technology talent. As a Bank of Tanzania-licensed PSP, we operate within Tanzania's financial regulatory framework.

• Deep NBC Domain Knowledge: Our CEO Victor Tesha spent years at NBC Bank, bringing first-hand understanding of NBC's culture, processes, credit workflows, and operational needs. This institutional knowledge accelerates requirements gathering, solution design, and change management.

• Banking Domain Expertise: Our leadership team brings direct experience from Tanzania's banking sector (NBC, Azania, UBA, ICB), providing deep understanding of local banking practices, credit processes, regulatory requirements, and customer expectations.

• Cost-Effective Delivery: As a local firm with lean operations, we deliver significant cost savings compared to international system integrators, while maintaining quality through our experienced team and proven methodology.

• Agile Delivery with Risk Control: Our phased approach delivers incremental business value within the 2-year timeline while maintaining controlled stage-gates, ensuring NBC has visibility and governance throughout the implementation.

• Long-Term Partnership: Located in Dar es Salaam, we are committed to providing sustained post-implementation support, continuous improvement, and technology refresh aligned with NBC's evolving business needs.
